domain: viniculture / wine forensics (terroir provenance via 87Sr/86Sr strontium-isotope + elemental fingerprint)
specific_mechanism: Wine provenance traces back to vineyard bedrock geology: 87Sr/86Sr ratios in soil transfer to vine via root uptake, then to grape and wine. The ratio is unique to the geological substratum, stable within a vineyard across vintages, and varies systematically across regions. Combined with other trace elements (e.g., rare-earth pattern, multi-element fingerprint) and IRMS isotopes (δ¹⁵N, δ¹³C), 2025 ML-on-geochemical-fingerprint methods authenticate sparkling-wine origin with high accuracy.
llm_application: Model provenance via 'training-data terroir': identify the specific dataset source of an LLM by detecting trace 'isotope' patterns in weight-space that are characteristic of that dataset's tokens/distribution. Information cascade: training imprints a multi-elemental fingerprint that survives fine-tuning, instruction-tuning, and RLHF (analogous to 87Sr/86Sr surviving from bedrock through vine to wine). Combine intrinsic fingerprint patterns (e.g., attention-parameter SD distributions per layer) with explicit watermarks to give cascade-style multi-stage provenance verification.
novelty_claim: Intrinsic LLM Fingerprint (2507.03014), Behavioral Fingerprint (2602.09434), Domain-specific watermarks (Gloaguen 2025), Semantically-conditioned watermarks (2505.16723), Watermarking+Fingerprinting Survey (3773028) all address model provenance. None specifically frames the multi-elemental wine-terroir signature (Sr+REE+IRMS) as a multi-layer-trace fingerprint where each 'trace element' is one type of intrinsic signature (attention SD, weight norm, activation pattern) and combined cascade-style.
